concepts.title: amortization type
concepts.definition: classifier of amortization algorithms

concepts.title: borrower monthly income
concepts.definition: total monthly qualifying income of a potential borrower

concepts.title: combined loan-to-value ratio
concepts.definition: ratio of the total amount of debt that is secured by the asset(s) and the appraised value of the asset(s) securing the financing

concepts.title: good faith estimate
concepts.definition: GFE - representation by the lender on the costs and implications of settlement (early termination) of the loan.

concepts.title: income
concepts.definition: revenue received during a period of time

concepts.title: loan payment schedule
concepts.definition: regular or explicit (ad hoc) payment schedule associated with a given loan-specific account

concepts.title: loan-to-value ratio
concepts.definition: ratio, expressed as a percentage, between the principal amount of the loan and the appraised value of the asset securing the financing

concepts.title: monetary amount
concepts.definition: measure that is an amount of money specified in monetary units

concepts.title: pre-payment terms
concepts.definition: principal repayment terms related to payment of the loan prior to maturity

concepts.title: total debt expense ratio
concepts.definition: ratio of all monthly debt payments of all borrowers, including proposed expenses, with respect to the income of the borrowers as relied upon to make a credit decision

concepts.title: total outstanding principal
concepts.definition: the principal balance of all loans secured by the property

concepts.title: loan application
concepts.definition: request by a potential borrower to a potential lender to borrow money containing information used to decide whether to grant the loan

concepts.title: loan application phase
concepts.definition: distinct phase in the lifecycle of a loan application, such as pre-qualification, application intake, underwriting, or decisioning

concepts.title: loan application status
concepts.definition: classifier indicating the state of a loan application at a particular point in its lifecycle, such as submitted, under review, approved, declined, or withdrawn

concepts.title: loan phase
concepts.definition: distinct phase in the lifecycle of a loan, such as origination, disbursement, repayment, or closure

concepts.title: pre-approval contract
concepts.definition: written commitment to lend when specified conditions are met, such as finding suitable property, and unchanged creditworthiness

concepts.title: pre-approval request
concepts.definition: request from a potential borrower that a lender commit to pre-approving the borrower for a loan of up to a specified amount of money

concepts.title: repayment phase
concepts.definition: phase in a loan's lifecycle during which the borrower repays outstanding principal and interest according to the loan's payment schedule

concepts.title: appraisal
concepts.definition: written estimate of the market value of something as of some point in time, typically provided by a qualified appraiser

concepts.title: appraised value
concepts.definition: estimated value of some asset as of a given point in time

concepts.title: collateral
concepts.definition: something pledged as security to ensure fulfillment of an obligation to another party, to lend money, extend credit, or provision securities

concepts.title: lender lien position
concepts.definition: classifier indicating whether the lender has the primary lien position with respect to an asset used as collateral for the loan

concepts.title: ownership interest
concepts.definition: classifier indicating the nature of the applicant's or borrower's ownership or leasehold interest in an asset used as collateral for the loan

concepts.title: property usage
concepts.definition: a category indicating the manner in which the borrower intends to utilize the property

concepts.title: real property
concepts.definition: physical asset defined as land together with any structures that are permanently attached to that land, such as houses, trees, fences and improvements

concepts.title: credit agreement
concepts.definition: contractual agreement in which a debtor receives something of value and typically agrees to repay the creditor by some date in the future, in some form (e.g., cash, securities, etc.), generally with interest

concepts.title: disclosure provision
concepts.definition: contractual provision that outlines the requirements and responsibilities of one or both parties to reveal certain information to each other

concepts.title: legal obligation
concepts.definition: an obligation or duty that is enforceable by a court

concepts.title: loan or credit account
concepts.definition: account associated with a service in which the account holder receives funds from the account provider under certain terms and conditions for repayment

concepts.title: payment
concepts.definition: delivery of money in fulfillment of an obligation, such as to satisfy a claim or debt

concepts.title: principal
concepts.definition: with respect to a debt: the value of an obligation, such as a bond or loan, raised and that must be repaid at maturity; for investments: the original amount of money invested, separate from any associated interest, dividends or capital gains

concepts.title: use of proceeds provision
concepts.definition: contract provision specifying how funds obtained through financing, such as through a credit agreement, offering, warrant, or other instrument are intended to be used

concepts.title: closed-end mortgage loan
concepts.definition: loan secured by real estate with no ability for the borrower to receive additional funds under the loan at a later date

concepts.title: collateralized loan
concepts.definition: secured loan that is secured with cash or other acceptable collateral (real property, securities or other assets) provided by the borrower as specified in the collateral agreement

concepts.title: home equity line of credit
concepts.definition: line of credit granted to a homeowner secured by the equity value in a borrower's home or other property

concepts.title: loan
concepts.definition: debt instrument whereby one party extends money or credit to another party (or parties) with the understanding that the borrowed money will be repaid according to the terms of the contract

concepts.title: loan offering
concepts.definition: offering related to a loan product that may be a tailored to particular circumstances, aimed at a group of borrowers or individual borrower

concepts.title: loan product
concepts.definition: financial product that is realized as a loan that a party may acquire from a lending institution with specific characteristics and terms

concepts.title: loan secured by real estate
concepts.definition: loan that, at origination, is secured wholly or substantially by a lien or liens on real property for which the lien or liens are central to the extension of the credit - that is, the borrower would not have been extended credit in the same amount or on terms as favorable without the lien or liens on real property

concepts.title: mortgage
concepts.definition: grant of financial interest in real property to a party that is not an owner of that real property and is recorded by a registration authority

concepts.title: mortgage product
concepts.definition: loan product, offered by a lender, that is realized as a mortgage — a loan secured by real property — with specific eligibility criteria, characteristics, and terms

concepts.title: open-end mortgage loan
concepts.definition: loan secured by real estate with a provision that the outstanding loan amount may be increased upon mutual agreement of the lender and the borrower

concepts.title: reverse mortgage loan
concepts.definition: loan secured by real estate that pays money to the borrower against a set principal limit based on the value of existing equity in the underlying collateral

concepts.title: secured loan
concepts.definition: loan in which the borrower pledges some asset via a security agreement as collateral for the loan, or that is secured via third-party guarantee

concepts.title: borrower
concepts.definition: party to a credit agreement that is obligated to repay the amount borrowed (principal) with interest and other fees according to the terms of the instrument

concepts.title: co-maker
concepts.definition: party that signs a borrower's promissory note, providing additional security and potentially improving the quality of the debt

concepts.title: creditor
concepts.definition: a party to whom an obligation, such as an amount of money, or good, or performance of some service exists

concepts.title: financial institution
concepts.definition: financial service provider identified as either a government agency or privately owned entity that collects funds from the public and from other institutions, and invests those funds in financial assets, such as loans, securities, bank deposits, and income-generating property

concepts.title: guarantor
concepts.definition: party that guarantees, endorses, or provides indemnity for some obligation on behalf of some other party

concepts.title: lender
concepts.definition: a party that extends credit or money to a borrower with the expectation of being repaid, usually with interest

concepts.title: mortgage indemnity guarantor
concepts.definition: guarantor and insurer that provides mortgage insurance in the form of a mortgage indemnity guarantee (MIG)

concepts.title: servicer
concepts.definition: party that collects principal and interest payments on behalf of the lender

concepts.title: loan purpose
concepts.definition: A selection of different types of loan purpose, being the purpose for which and manner in which loan (credit) draw-down amounts are to be used. This shows the purpose for which credit is to be used, and implies certain kinds of fact that relate to that specific type of loan e.g. mortgages. These are also identified as tranche types in tranches of a credit facility.

concepts.title: mortgage loan purpose
concepts.definition: the purpose for which mortgage loan proceeds will be used, such as real property purchase, dwelling construction, or loan refinancing

concepts.title: HMDA covered loan contract
concepts.definition: a closed-end mortgage loan or open-end line of credit that is not an excluded transaction for HMDA reporting under US section 1003.3(c) of the Revised Home Mortgage Disclosure Act of 2015

concepts.title: HMDA report
concepts.definition: a report prepared to satisfy HMDA regulatory reporting requirements as described US section 1003.3(c) of the Revised Home Mortgage Disclosure Act of 2015

concepts.title: Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry Identifier
concepts.definition: the number permanently assigned by the Nationwide Mortgage Licensing System and Registry (NMLS) for each company, branch, and individual that maintains a single account on NMLS.

concepts.title: borrower disclosure requirement
concepts.definition: regulatory requirement that a lender disclose specified information to a borrower or prospective borrower — such as the terms and costs of a loan product — conferring a corresponding right to that information and governing how the borrower is assessed

concepts.title: consumer credit protection law
concepts.definition: consumer protection law that regulates the extension of credit to consumers — governing matters such as disclosure of terms and costs, fair lending, and borrower rights (for example, in the US, the Consumer Credit Protection Act and its implementing regulations)

concepts.title: loan regulatory requirement
concepts.definition: A regulatory requirement defined in regulations by a comsumer credit act or other legislation.

concepts.title: reg b
concepts.definition: US regulation concerning "Equal credit opportunity act" Electronic delivery of disclosures

concepts.title: reg z
concepts.definition: US Fed regulation "Truth in Lending Act" uniform standards for electronic delivery of disclosures

concepts.title: universal loan identifier
concepts.definition: unique identifier given to unequivocally identify a specific loan secured by real estate

concepts.title: automated underwriting system
concepts.definition: software system that collects the information necessary to approve a loan application and supports a mortgage lender's analysis of a new loan application

concepts.title: borrower assessment
concepts.definition: assessment report detailing information about the borrower and their credit history that may be relevant to the loan application

concepts.title: collateral valuation
concepts.definition: assessment activity resulting in the valuation of real property as collateral

concepts.title: credit risk assessment
concepts.definition: risk assessment that focuses on determining the likelihood of a potential borrower repaying a loan

concepts.title: income verification report
concepts.definition: report providing the methodology and supporting documentation used to verify the borrower's income

concepts.title: individual person credit rating
concepts.definition: credit rating that provides an opinion of the creditworthiness of a person

concepts.title: payment history
concepts.definition: record of actual payments of principal, interest, and other related amounts made by a borrower to a lender or servicer in order to fulfill their re-payment obligation

concepts.title: underwriting decision
concepts.definition: classifier providing a loan approval recommendation determined either manually or by an automated underwriting system

bridges.rationale: HMDA requires every covered loan/application to be recorded on the institution's Loan/Application Register that constitutes the HMDA report. FIBO models both HMDA-CoveredLoanContract and HMDA-Report but does not connect the reported loan to the report that reports it.
bridges.citation: 12 CFR 1003.4 (Regulation C — compilation of reportable data)

bridges.rationale: A HMDA report is filed by a covered financial institution. FIBO links HMDA-Report to the RegulatoryAgency it is submitted-to, but not to the filing institution — leaving the filer unresolved.
For KYC / entity resolution a financial institution is identified by its Legal Entity Identifier. FIBO defines LegalEntityIdentifier in BE but does not connect FinancialInstitution to it.

Lender is modeled as a party role (is-a ContractParty/Creditor); FinancialInstitution is the legal-entity type that plays it. FIBO leaves the role-to-entity link implicit, blocking resolution of 'the lender' to a regulated institution.
bridges.citation: FIBO FBC party-role vs entity modeling
